pgala_token_legacy.role: Legacy pGALA token on BSC (deprecated after the incident; pNetwork re-issued under a fresh address).

pgala_token_relaunch.role: Re-issued GALA token on BSC after the incident (out of fuzzer scope; documented for completeness).

root_cause_summary.vulnerable_pattern: Bridge custodian was re-deployed on BSC without rotating the legacy ECDSA signing key. The legacy custodian remained authoritative for mint() and an attacker who held a stale key signed unbacked mints.
root_cause_summary.verification_bypass: ecrecover(digest, v, r, s) returned the legacy signer for attacker-supplied signatures, so the require(recovered == signer) check passed without any cross-chain consistency.
root_cause_summary.notes: pNetwork retired the legacy custodian within ~hours of the incident and re-issued GALA under 0x7dDE.... The benchmark models only the original custodian; the re-issuance flow is out of scope.

exploit_characteristics.notes: A single mint transaction emitted ~1 billion pGALA to the attacker, after which the tokens were dumped on PancakeSwap. PancakeSwap-side liquidity drain is documented in the post-mortem but kept out of the fuzzer scope.

references.title: pNetwork — pGALA on BSC Token Incident (2022-11-03)
